reasoning: The Nominal Broad U.S. Dollar Index has surged sharply in recent days ahead of the March 17-18, 2026 FOMC meeting, climbing from 118.7 on Mar 10 to 120.55 on Mar 13. This massive 1.5% jump indicates that the market has strongly priced in a hawkish Fed hold (CME FedWatch indicates a 92% chance of a hold). With such a steep, consensus rally leading directly into a major event, the risk becomes asymmetric to the downside due to 'buy the rumor, sell the news' dynamics. Any surprisingly dovish tone or neutral Summary of Economic Projections (dot plot) could spark profit-taking. Statistically, the FRED linear model anticipates a mean-reverting drop to 119.7 by the resolution date, projecting only a 29% probability of the index staying above the current 120.6 level based on trend dynamics, counterbalanced slightly by seasonal strength. Overall, a retracement following this pre-FOMC spike makes a week-over-week increase less likely than a decrease.
